meaning: n.时尚,时髦,风气,流行,风行adj.流行的,时髦的
examples: Pale colours are much more in vogue than autumnal bronzes and coppers.
Indigenous foods like salt marsh lamb are in vogue.
In 1947, Vogue magazine published many food photos taken by Penn.
Sometimes there are a great many when a particular technology or business idea is in vogue.
Mosbacher's sister Lyn Paulsin accompanies her, while Vogue editor Anna Wintour brings her daughter, Bee Shaffer.
